title: Hungarian Pronunciation Guide for English Speakers
content_html:
Hungarian Pronunciation Guide for English Speakers

Hungarian pronunciation can seem intimidating at first with its unique sounds and accent marks, but the good news is that Hungarian spelling is remarkably consistent. Once you learn the rules, you can pronounce any Hungarian word correctly just by reading it. This guide will walk you through every sound in the Hungarian language.

💕

Phrase to Learn

Egészségedre

Cheers / Bless you

[ E-gés-shé-ged-re ]

Used when toasting or when someone sneezes - a great word to practice Hungarian sounds

The Hungarian Alphabet

Hungarian uses the Latin alphabet with several additional letters. The full alphabet has 44 letters, including digraphs (two-letter combinations that make one sound).

Letter Sound Example Word Meaning
A "o" as in "hot" alma apple
Á "a" as in "father" ház house
B "b" as in "boy" bolt shop
C "ts" as in "cats" cica kitten
CS "ch" as in "church" csokoládé chocolate
D "d" as in "dog" dél south/noon
DZ "dz" as in "adze" edz trains
DZS "j" as in "jungle" dzsungel jungle
E "e" as in "bed" ember human
É "ay" as in "say" élet life
F "f" as in "fun" fa tree
G "g" as in "go" gép machine
GY soft "d" + "y" gyerek child
H "h" as in "hello" ház house
I "i" as in "bit" isten god
Í "ee" as in "see" íj bow
J "y" as in "yes" jó good
K "k" as in "kite" kutya dog
L "l" as in "love" lány girl
LY "y" as in "yes" lyuk hole
M "m" as in "mom" mond says
N "n" as in "no" nagy big
NY "ny" as in "canyon" nyár summer
O "o" as in "or" otthon home
Ó long "o" as in "go" óra hour/clock
Ö like German "ö" öröm joy
Ő long version of ö ők they
P "p" as in "pen" piros red
R rolled "r" reggel morning
S "sh" as in "ship" sok many
SZ "s" as in "sun" szép beautiful
T "t" as in "top" tél winter
TY soft "t" + "y" tyúk hen
U "u" as in "put" utat road
Ú "oo" as in "food" út path
Ü like German "ü" ül sits
Ű long version of ü fű grass
V "v" as in "van" virág flower
Z "z" as in "zoo" zene music
ZS "zh" as in "measure" zseb pocket
🇭🇺

Spelling Is Pronunciation

Unlike English, Hungarian spelling is phonetic. Each letter or letter combination always makes the same sound. Once you master the alphabet, you can pronounce any Hungarian word correctly, even if you've never seen it before!

Understanding Hungarian Vowels

Hungarian vowels are divided into two categories: short and long. The accent mark (´) indicates a long vowel, and the double dots (¨ or ˝) create different vowel sounds entirely.

Short Vowels

a Short 'o' sound

Pronunciation: Like 'o' in 'hot'

"Alma (apple) - AHL-mah"

e Short 'e' sound

Pronunciation: Like 'e' in 'bed'

"Ember (human) - EM-ber"

i Short 'i' sound

Pronunciation: Like 'i' in 'bit'

"Mit (what) - mit"

o Short 'o' sound

Pronunciation: Like 'o' in 'or'

"Otthon (home) - OT-ton"

u Short 'u' sound

Pronunciation: Like 'u' in 'put'

"Kutya (dog) - KU-tyah"

Long Vowels

Short Long Pronunciation Difference Example
a á "o" in hot → "a" in father anya (mother) vs ára (its price)
e é "e" in bed → "ay" in say kerek (round) vs kérek (I ask)
i í "i" in bit → "ee" in see mit (what) vs mít (myth)
o ó short "o" → long "o" as in "go" kor (age) vs kór (disease)
u ú "u" in put → "oo" in food kuk (peek) vs kúp (cone)

The Tricky Ones: Ö, Ő, Ü, Ű

These vowels don't exist in English but are common in German. Here's how to make them:

ö Round your lips like 'o', say 'e'

Pronunciation: Similar to 'ur' in 'fur'

"Örül (is happy) - Ö-rül"

ő Long version of ö

Pronunciation: Hold the ö sound longer

"Ő (he/she) - Ő"

ü Round your lips like 'u', say 'i'

Pronunciation: Like French 'tu' or German 'über'

"Ül (sits) - ül"

ű Long version of ü

Pronunciation: Hold the ü sound longer

"Fű (grass) - fű"

🇭🇺

Practice Tip

To pronounce ö and ü correctly, try this: Make the shape of your mouth as if you're going to say "oo," but then try to say "ee" without moving your lips. The resulting sound is close to ü. For ö, do the same but aim for an "eh" sound.

Consonant Combinations

Hungarian has several consonant combinations that are treated as single letters. These are crucial to master.

The S Sounds

This is often confusing for English speakers:

Hungarian Sound English Example
S "sh" ship
SZ "s" sun
ZS "zh" measure
szép beautiful

Pronunciation: sayp (NOT shayp!)

"Nagyon szép vagy. (You are very beautiful.)"

sok many/much

Pronunciation: shok (NOT sok!)

"Sok szerencsét! (Good luck!)"

The Soft Consonants: GY, NY, TY

These sounds are made by pressing your tongue against the roof of your mouth.

gy Soft 'd' + 'y'

Pronunciation: Like 'd' in 'dew' (British)

"Magyar (Hungarian) - MAH-dyar"

ny Like Spanish 'ñ'

Pronunciation: Like 'ny' in 'canyon'

"Nyár (summer) - nyahr"

ty Soft 't' + 'y'

Pronunciation: Like 't' in 'tune' (British)

"Kutya (dog) - KU-tyah"

The C Sounds

Hungarian Sound Example
C "ts" cats
CS "ch" church
cica kitten

Pronunciation: TSI-tsah

"A cica alszik. (The kitten is sleeping.)"

csokoládé chocolate

Pronunciation: CHO-ko-lah-day

"Szereted a csokoládét? (Do you like chocolate?)"

Stress and Rhythm

Hungarian stress is simple: the emphasis ALWAYS falls on the first syllable, regardless of word length.

Word Pronunciation Meaning
SZE-re-tek SZE is stressed I love
BA-rá-tom BA is stressed my friend
MA-gyar-or-szág MA is stressed Hungary
SZE-ret-lek SZE is stressed I love you
GYÖ-nyö-rű GYÖ is stressed beautiful
🇭🇺

Always First Syllable

No matter how long a Hungarian word is, stress always falls on the first syllable. This is much simpler than English, where stress can fall on different syllables. The word "köszönöm" (thank you) is pronounced KÖ-sö-nöm, with the emphasis clearly on KÖ.

The Rolled R

Hungarian uses a rolled or trilled R, similar to Spanish or Italian.

r Rolled R

Pronunciation: Tip of tongue vibrates against roof of mouth

"Reggel (morning) - REG-gel (with rolled R)"

Practice words with R:

Word Pronunciation Meaning
Rózsa RÓ-zhah rose
Reggel REG-gel morning
Rendben REND-ben okay
Város VAH-rosh city
Szeret SE-ret loves

Common Pronunciation Mistakes

Here are errors English speakers often make:

Mistake 1: Pronouncing S as "s"

Wrong: "szeretlek" as "seretlek" Correct: "szeretlek" as "SEH-ret-lek" (sz = s sound)

Mistake 2: Not distinguishing a and á

Wrong: Treating both as the same "a" Correct: a = "o" in hot; á = "a" in father

Mistake 3: Stressing the wrong syllable

Wrong: Putting stress on the last syllable Correct: Stress is ALWAYS on the first syllable

Mistake 4: Pronouncing gy as "g-y"

Wrong: "magyar" as "mag-yar" Correct: "magyar" as "MAH-dyar" (gy is ONE soft sound)

Practice Sentences

Try pronouncing these common phrases:

Köszönöm szépen Thank you very much

Pronunciation: KÖ-sö-nöm SAY-pen

"Say this when someone helps you"

Hogy vagy? How are you?

Pronunciation: HODY vahdy

"Informal greeting to friends and partners"

Nem értem I don't understand

Pronunciation: Nem AIR-tem

"Useful when you need help"

Szeretlek, drágám I love you, my dear

Pronunciation: SE-ret-lek DRAH-gahm

"A romantic expression for your partner"

Pronunciation Practice Chart

Use this quick reference when practicing:

Challenge Letter(s) Sound Practice Word
S vs SZ S "sh" sok (many)
SZ "s" szép (beautiful)
Soft consonants GY soft "d-y" magyar
NY "ny" nyár
TY soft "t-y" kutya
Rounded vowels Ö rounded "e" örül
Ü rounded "i" ül
Long vowels Á "ah" drága
É "ay" szép
Í "ee" íj
🇭🇺

Be Patient With Yourself

Hungarian pronunciation takes time to master, but Hungarians are incredibly appreciative when foreigners make the effort to learn their language. Don't be afraid to ask native speakers to correct your pronunciation. They'll be happy to help!

Tips for Improving Your Pronunciation

  1. Listen actively - Watch Hungarian movies, listen to Hungarian music, and pay attention to how sounds are formed.

  2. Record yourself - Compare your pronunciation to native speakers and identify areas for improvement.

  3. Practice with your partner - If you have a Hungarian-speaking partner, ask them to correct you gently.

  4. Focus on one sound at a time - Master the ö before moving to ü, and so on.

  5. Use tongue twisters - Hungarian tongue twisters (nyelvtörők) are excellent for practice.

Hungarian Tongue Twister

Egy kupac kopasz kukac A pile of bald maggots

Pronunciation: Edy KU-pahts KO-pahs KU-kahts

"A classic Hungarian tongue twister to practice K sounds"

Quick Reference Summary

Remember these key rules:

  1. Stress: Always on the first syllable
  2. S = "sh": Never the English "s" sound
  3. SZ = "s": This is the English "s" sound
  4. Accent marks: Change vowel length (á, é, í, ó, ú) or quality (ö, ő, ü, ű)
  5. Digraphs: Two-letter combinations (cs, gy, ly, ny, sz, ty, zs) are single sounds
  6. Rolled R: Like Spanish or Italian
  7. Consistent spelling: Letters always make the same sound

With practice and patience, you'll be pronouncing Hungarian beautifully. Your Hungarian-speaking loved ones will appreciate every effort you make!

Related Articles

  • Hungarian Baby & Pregnancy Vocabulary: Essential Words for Expecting Couples
  • 50 Hungarian Date Night Vocabulary Words to Impress Your Partner
  • Hungarian Grammar Basics: Simple Rules for Romantic Conversations
  • Hungarian Restaurant Vocabulary: Dining Out With Your Partner
  • Is Hungarian Hard to Learn? An Honest Guide

title: Hungarian Proposal & Engagement Phrases: How to Pop the Question
content_html:
Proposing in your partner's native language is one of the most romantic gestures you can make. Whether you're planning an elaborate lánykérés or a simple, heartfelt moment, knowing how to express your intentions in Hungarian will make this milestone unforgettable. This guide covers everything from the perfect proposal phrases to announcing your eljegyzés to family and friends.

For more romantic expressionsu/hungarian-flirting-phrases-for-english-speakers/">romantic expressions, see our guide on Hungarian pet names and terms of endearment.

💕

Phrase to Learn

Hozzám jössz feleségül?

Will you marry me?

[ hohz-zahm yuss fe-le-shay-guel? ]

The most important question you'll ever ask.

The Proposal Vocabulary

Master these essential words before your big moment:

gyűrű ring

Pronunciation: dyue-rue

"A jegygyűrű gyönyörűen csillog."

Hungarian English Context
lánykérés proposal The act of proposing
eljegyzés engagement The period before marriage
gyűrű ring The engagement ring
vőlegény fiancé Engaged man
menyasszony fiancée Engaged woman
igen yes The answer you're hoping for
örökké forever For eternity
életem szerelme love of my life Romantic declaration
🇭🇺

Proposal Traditions

In Hungarian-speaking cultures, proposal traditions may include asking the parents for their blessing beforehand. This traditional gesture is still valued by many families and can make a wonderful impression.

Ways to Propose

Choosing the perfect words for your lánykérés requires thoughtfulness and preparation. While "Hozzám jössz feleségül?" is the traditional question, you can personalize your proposal by adding context that reflects your unique relationship. Consider the setting where you'll propose – whether it's during a quiet evening at home after preparing dinner together, or perhaps after a playful game night where you've both been laughing and enjoying each other's company.

Many couples find that incorporating shared memories into the proposal makes it more meaningful. You might reference the first time you met, a special trip you took together, or even an inside joke that only the two of you understand. The key is to speak from the heart while using Hungarian words that show your commitment to learning and honoring your partner's language and culture.

szerelem love

Pronunciation: SE-re-lem

"Te vagy az életem szerelme"

boldogság happiness

Pronunciation: BOL-dog-shag

"Te vagy az én boldogságom"

The Classic Proposal

"Hozzám jössz feleségül?" - Will you marry me?

This simple, direct question is perfect for any setting. Practice the pronunciation until it feels natural.

More Romantic Variations

  • "You are the életem szerelme. Hozzám jössz feleségül?" - Adding a declaration of love
  • "I want to spend örökké with you." - Building to the question
  • "Will you make me the happiest person in the world?" - An alternative approach
jegyes engaged

Pronunciation: ye-dyesh

"Mi jegyesek vagyunk!"

Announcing Your Engagement

Once your partner says "igen!", you'll want to share the news:

To Family

  • "We're jegyes!" - The joyful announcement
  • "vőlegény/menyasszony said igen!" - Sharing the excitement
  • "We have wonderful news!" - Building anticipation

To Friends

  • "Guess what happened?" - Creating suspense
  • "Look at my gyűrű!" - Showing off the ring
  • "We're getting married!" - Direct and joyful

On Social Media

  • "She/He said igen!" - Classic announcement
  • "örökké starts now!" - Romantic caption
  • "jegyes to my életem szerelme" - Heartfelt declaration
🇭🇺

Engagement Celebrations

In many Hungarian-speaking families, an eljegyzés party is held to celebrate the couple. This gathering allows both families to meet and celebrate the upcoming union together.

Ring Vocabulary

The gyűrű is central to the lánykérés:

Hungarian English Description
gyűrű ring The piece of jewelry
Engagement gyűrű engagement ring The symbol of commitment
Diamond diamond Popular gemstone
Gold gold Common metal
Fit fit How the ring sits

Preparing Your Proposal

Successful proposals require more than just knowing the right words – they demand careful preparation and attention to detail. Start by practicing your pronunciation weeks before the big day. Record yourself saying "Hozzám jössz feleségül?" and listen back to ensure clarity. You might feel self-conscious at first, but remember that your effort to speak Hungarian will mean the world to your partner.

Consider the practical aspects of your proposal moment. Will you be kneeling? If so, practice getting down on one knee while keeping the gyűrű box secure and accessible. Think about whether you want to incorporate any Hungarian traditions, such as presenting flowers or asking for a family blessing beforehand. Some couples enjoy visiting a meaningful location together, like the salon where they once had their hair cut side by side, or a favorite spot where they played board games during their courtship.

Timing is crucial – choose a moment when you're both relaxed and present. Avoid proposing when either of you is stressed, tired, or distracted by everyday tasks like laundry or cleaning. The proposal should feel special and intentional, not rushed or convenience-based. If you're feeling nervous about forgetting your words, write them down on a small card to keep in your pocket as a backup.

meglepetés surprise

Pronunciation: MEG-le-pe-tesh

"Ez egy nagy meglepetés"

Practice Tips

Moment What to Say Translation

Purpose Phrase

Your lánykérés in Hungarian will be a story you'll tell örökké. Take your time, speak from the heart, and let your love shine through every word.

title: Hungarian Restaurant Vocabulary: Dining Out With Your Partner
content_html:
Hungarian Restaurant Vocabulary: Dining Out With Your Partner

Planning a romantic dinner date with your Hungarian partner? Whether you're visiting Hungary or dining at a Hungarian restaurant abroad, knowing the right vocabulary will make your experience more enjoyable and impressive. This guide covers everything from making reservations to paying the bill.

💕

Phrase to Learn

Jó étvágyat!

Enjoy your meal!

[ Yó AYT-vah-dyaht ]

Said before eating, similar to 'Bon appétit' - you'll hear this from servers and can say it to your partner

Making a Reservation

Before you arrive, you might want to book a table. Here are the essential phraseen/hu/meeting-your-partner-s-family/">essential phrasesn/hu/hungarian-video-call-phrases-family/">essential phrasesn/hu/hungarian-small-talk-conversation-starters/">essential phrasesn/hu/hungarian-long-distance-relationship-phrases/">essential phrasesn/hu/hungarian-how-to-apologize-phrases-for-couples/">essential phrasesn/hu/hungarian-good-morning-goodnight-phrases/">essential phrasesn/hu/hungarian-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">essential phrasesn/hu/essential-hungarian-phrases-for-couples/">essential phrases:

Szeretnék asztalt foglalni I would like to book a table

Pronunciation: SE-ret-nayk AHS-tahlt FOG-lahl-ni

"Szeretnék asztalt foglalni két főre."

Két főre For two people

Pronunciation: Kayt FŐ-re

"Kérek egy asztalt két főre."

Ma estére For tonight

Pronunciation: Mah ESH-té-re

"Szeretnék asztalt foglalni ma estére."

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Holnapra For tomorrow HOL-nahp-rah
Szombat estére For Saturday evening SOM-baht ESH-té-re
Nyolc órára For 8 o'clock Nyolts Ó-rah-rah
Van szabad asztaluk? Do you have a free table? Vahn SAH-bahd AHS-tah-luk
Ablak melletti asztalt A table by the window AHB-lahk MEL-let-ti AHS-tahlt
🇭🇺

Reservation Tips

In Budapest, popular restaurants often require reservations, especially on weekends. It's common to call ahead or use online booking platforms. For romantic restaurants, mention if you're celebrating a special occasion - Hungarian restaurants often add special touches for couples.

Arriving at the Restaurant

When you arrive, here's how to navigate the initial interaction:

Van foglalásunk We have a reservation

Pronunciation: Vahn FOG-lah-lah-shunk

"Jó estét, van foglalásunk két főre."

Ülhetünk kint? Can we sit outside?

Pronunciation: ÜL-he-tünk kint

"Szép az idő. Ülhetünk kint?"

Phrase Translation Pronunciation
Jó estét kívánok Good evening (formal) Yó ESH-tayt KÍ-vah-nok
A nevem... My name is... Ah NE-vem
Dohányzó vagy nemdohányzó? Smoking or non-smoking? DO-hahny-zó vahdy NEM-do-hahny-zó
Nemdohányzó, kérem Non-smoking, please NEM-do-hahny-zó KAY-rem
Erre tessék This way, please ER-re TESH-shayk

Understanding the Menu

The Hungarian menu (étlap) is typically organized into sections:

Étlap Menu

Pronunciation: AYT-lahp

"Kérhetem az étlapot?"

Menu Sections

Hungarian English What to Expect
Előételek Appetizers Soups, starters
Levesek Soups Hungarian soups are hearty
Főételek Main courses Meat dishes, traditional fare
Köretek Side dishes Potatoes, vegetables
Saláták Salads Fresh salads
Desszertek Desserts Sweet treats
Italok Beverages Drinks
Borlap Wine list Hungarian wines
Mi a mai ajánlat? What's today's special?

Pronunciation: Mi ah MAH-i AH-yahn-laht

"Mi a mai ajánlat? Mit javasol?"

🇭🇺

Traditional Hungarian Menu Items

Look for classic dishes like gulyás (goulash), halászlé (fisherman's soup), pörkölt (stew), paprikás csirke (chicken paprikash), and túrós csusza (pasta with cottage cheese). For dessert, try Somlói galuska or Dobos torta!

Ordering Food

These phrases will help you communicate with your server:

Rendelhetünk? Can we order?

Pronunciation: REN-del-he-tünk

"Elnézést, rendelhetünk?"

Mit ajánl? What do you recommend?

Pronunciation: Mit AH-yahnl

"Mit ajánl a mai specialitások közül?"

Ezt kérem I'll have this one

Pronunciation: Est KAY-rem

"Ezt kérem, köszönöm."

Common Ordering Phrases

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Számomra... For me... SAH-mom-rah
A párom részére... For my partner... Ah PAH-rom RAY-sé-re
Kérek egy... I'd like a... KAY-rek edy
Szeretnék... I would like... SE-ret-nayk
Még gondolkodunk We're still thinking Mayg GON-dol-ko-dunk
Választottunk We've decided VAH-lahs-tot-tunk
Van vegetáriánus menüjük? Do you have a vegetarian menu?

Pronunciation: Vahn ve-ge-TAH-ri-ah-nush ME-nü-yük

"Van vegetáriánus menüjük vagy gluténmentes ételük?"

Food Allergies and Dietary Restrictions

Important phrases if you have dietary needs:

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Allergiás vagyok... I'm allergic to... AHL-ler-gi-ahsh VAH-dyok
...mogyoróra ...nuts MO-dyo-ró-rah
...tejre ...dairy TEY-re
...gluténre ...gluten GLU-tayn-re
...tojásra ...eggs TO-yahsh-rah
Vegetáriánus vagyok I'm vegetarian ve-ge-TAH-ri-ah-nush VAH-dyok
Vegán vagyok I'm vegan VE-gahn VAH-dyok
Van ebben...? Does this contain...? Vahn EB-ben

Ordering Drinks

Hungary is famous for its wines and pálinka (fruit brandy).

Egy pohár bort kérek I'd like a glass of wine

Pronunciation: Edy PO-hahr bort KAY-rek

"Egy pohár vörös bort kérek."

Az itallapot kérem The drinks menu, please

Pronunciation: Ahz I-tahl-lah-pot KAY-rem

"Először az itallapot kérem."

Drink Vocabulary

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Víz Water Veez
Szénsavas víz Sparkling water SAYN-shah-vahsh veez
Szénsavmentes víz Still water SAYN-sahv-men-tesh veez
Sör Beer Shör
Fehér bor White wine FE-hayr bor
Vörös bor Red wine VÖ-rösh bor
Rozé Rosé RO-zay
Pezsgő Champagne/Sparkling wine PEZ-gő
Kávé Coffee KAH-vay
Tea Tea TE-ah
Pálinka Fruit brandy PAH-lin-kah
🇭🇺

Hungarian Wine Regions

Hungary has excellent wines! Ask for Tokaji (sweet wine), Egri Bikavér (Bull's Blood - a red blend), or wines from Villány. Saying "Egészségedre!" (To your health!) before drinking is customary. Never clink beer glasses - it's a Hungarian superstition dating back to 1848.

During the Meal

Phrases you might need while eating:

Nagyon finom Very delicious

Pronunciation: NAH-dyon FI-nom

"Nagyon finom ez a pörkölt!"

Kérhetek még egy kis kenyeret? Could I have some more bread?

Pronunciation: KAYR-he-tek mayg edy kish KE-nye-ret

"Elnézést, kérhetek még egy kis kenyeret?"

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Jó étvágyat! Enjoy your meal! Yó AYT-vah-dyaht
Egészségedre! Cheers! (To your health) E-gays-shay-ged-re
Kérek egy kis sót Some salt, please KAY-rek edy kish shót
Borsot kérek Pepper, please BOR-shot KAY-rek
Szalvétát kérek A napkin, please SAHL-vay-taht KAY-rek
Hol van a mosdó? Where's the restroom? Hol vahn ah MOSH-dó
Megkóstolhatom a tiédet? Can I taste yours?

Pronunciation: MEG-kósh-tol-hah-tom ah TI-ay-det

"Ez nagyon jól néz ki. Megkóstolhatom a tiédet?"

Romantic Dinner Phrases

Make your date night special with these romantic additions:

Gyönyörű helyet választottál You chose a beautiful place

Pronunciation: DYÖN-yö-rű HE-yet VAH-lahs-tot-tahl

"Köszönöm a vacsorát. Gyönyörű helyet választottál."

Örülök, hogy itt vagyunk együtt I'm happy we're here together

Pronunciation: Ö-rü-lök hody itt VAH-dyunk E-dyütt

"Nagyon örülök, hogy itt vagyunk együtt ma este."

Romantic Phrase Translation When to Use
Gyertyafényes vacsorát kérek A candlelit dinner, please When making reservation
Ez egy különleges este This is a special evening During dinner
Köszönöm ezt az estét Thank you for this evening End of dinner
Szeretlek I love you Anytime!
Nagyon boldog vagyok veled I'm very happy with you Romantic moment

Paying the Bill

When you're ready to leave:

A számlát kérem The bill, please

Pronunciation: Ah SAHM-laht KAY-rem

"Elnézést, a számlát kérem."

Fizethetünk? Can we pay?

Pronunciation: FI-zet-he-tünk

"Elnézést, fizethetünk?"

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Fizetek én I'll pay FI-ze-tek ayn
Külön fizetünk We'll pay separately KÜ-lön FI-ze-tünk
Együtt fizetünk We'll pay together E-dyütt FI-ze-tünk
Bankkártyával By card BAHNK-kahr-tyah-vahl
Készpénzzel With cash KAYS-paynz-zel
Stimmel It's correct SHTIM-mel
Benne van a borravaló Is the tip included? BEN-ne vahn ah BOR-rah-vah-ló
🇭🇺

Tipping in Hungary

Tipping is customary in Hungary, typically 10-15% of the bill. When paying, you tell the server how much you want to pay in total (including tip). For example, if the bill is 8,000 Ft, you might say "Kilencezret kérek" (9,000, please) to include a tip.

Traditional Hungarian Dishes to Try

Impress your partner by ordering these Hungarian classics:

Dish Description Pronunciation
Gulyás Classic goulash soup GU-lyahsh
Halászlé Spicy fisherman's soup HAH-lahs-lay
Paprikás csirke Chicken in paprika sauce PAHP-ri-kahsh CHIR-ke
Pörkölt Hungarian stew PÖR-költ
Töltött káposzta Stuffed cabbage TÖL-tött KAH-pos-tah
Lángos Fried dough with toppings LAHN-gosh
Túrós csusza Pasta with cottage cheese TÚ-rósh CHU-sah

Desserts

Dessert Description Pronunciation
Somlói galuska Trifle with chocolate sauce SHOM-ló-i GAH-lush-kah
Dobos torta Layered sponge cake DO-bosh TOR-tah
Kürtőskalács Chimney cake KÜR-tősh-kah-lahch
Rétes Strudel RAY-tesh
Palacsinta Crepes PAH-lah-chin-tah

Sample Conversation at a Restaurant

Here's a typical restaurant exchange:

Server: Jó estét kívánok! Van foglalásuk? Good evening! Do you have a reservation?

You: Igen, két főre, a nevem Smith. Yes, for two, the name is Smith.

Server: Persze, erre tessék. Íme az étlap. Of course, this way please. Here's the menu.

You: Köszönjük. Mit ajánl ma este? Thank you. What do you recommend tonight?

Server: A mai ajánlatunk a paprikás csirke. Nagyon finom! Today's special is chicken paprikash. It's very delicious!

You: Nagyszerű! Ezt kérem. A párom részére a pörköltet kérjük. Great! I'll have that. For my partner, we'll have the pörkölt.

Server: Italból mit parancsolnak? What would you like to drink?

You: Egy üveg egri bikavért kérünk, köszönjük. We'd like a bottle of Bull's Blood, thank you.

At the end of the meal:

You: A számlát kérem. Nagyon finom volt! The bill, please. It was very delicious!

Server: Köszönjük szépen! Összesen 15,000 forint. Thank you very much! The total is 15,000 forints.

You: Kérek tizenhétezret. Köszönjük a nagyszerű estét! Make it 17,000. Thank you for the wonderful evening!

Essential Phrases

Sajnálom (SHOY-nah-lohm) is the most important phrase to know. Use it whenever you need to make conversation.

Cultural Tip

In Hungarian culture, small talk often touches on family and personal wellbeing before moving to other topics. Showing genuine interest in someone's family is appreciated.

More Useful Expressions

Discussing the Surroundings and Weather

title: Hungarian Emotional Support Phrases: Comfort Your Partner in Difficult Times
content_html:
When your Hungarian-speaking partner faces difficult times, offering comfort in their native language creates profound connection. Your effort to learn Hungarian shows exceptional love and dedication.

Phrases of Presence

Itt vagyok neked (It va-jok ne-ked) I'm here for you

Nem vagy egyedül ebben (Nem vaj e-je-dewl eb-ben) You're not alone in this

Mindig számíthatsz rám (Min-dig sa-meet-hats ram) You can always count on me

Expressing Empathy

Megértem, min mész keresztül (Meg-air-tem, min mais ke-res-tewl) I understand what you're going through

Teljesen normális így érezni (Tel-ye-shen nor-ma-lish eej air-ez-ni) It's completely normal to feel this way

Az érzéseid teljesen jogosak Your feelings are completely valid

Offering Comfort

Minden rendben lesz (Min-den rend-ben les) Everything will be okay

Együtt meg fogjuk oldani (E-jewt meg fog-yuk ol-da-ni) Together we'll solve it

Nagyon büszke vagyok rád (Na-jyon bews-ke va-jok rad) I'm very proud of you

Active Support

Mit tehetek, hogy segítsek? (Mit te-he-tek, hoj she-geet-shek?) What can I do to help?

Beszélni szeretnél róla, vagy csak együtt legyünk? Do you want to talk about it or just be together?

Mondd el, mire van szükséged Tell me what you need

Specific Situations

Different difficult situations require tailored responses in Hungarian. Understanding the nuances between grief, stress, anxiety, and disappointment helps you offer appropriate comfort. Hungarian culture values directness combined with warmth, so your support phrases should be both genuine and clear.

When someone experiences loss, Hungarian has specific phrases that acknowledge the depth of pain without minimizing it. These expressions show respect for the grieving process while offering tangible support. In Hungarian culture, being present matters more than finding perfect words.

vigasz comfort, consolation

Pronunciation: VEE-goss

"A vigaszod sokat jelent nekem."

megértés understanding

Pronunciation: MEG-ayr-taysh

"Köszönöm a megértésedet."

Stress and overwhelm require a different approach. Hungarian phrases for these situations often emphasize taking things step by step and remind your partner of their inner strength. The language has beautiful expressions that combine practical support with emotional encouragement.

Grief

Nagyon sajnálom a veszteségedet I'm so sorry for your loss

Stress

Lélegezz velem együtt Breathe with me

Lépésről lépésre haladjunk Let's go step by step

Disappointment

Tudom, mennyit jelentett ez neked I know how much this meant to you

Nem kell mindig erősnek lenned You don't always have to be strong

Physical Comfort

Gyere ide, hadd öleljelek meg Come here, let me hug you

Szeretnél egy ölelést? Do you want a hug?

Encouragement

Erősebb vagy, mint gondolnád You're stronger than you think

Hiszek benned I believe in you

Meg fogod csinálni You'll make it

Being Present

Nem kell mondanod semmit You don't have to say anything

Csak itt akarok lenni veled I just want to be here with you

Your effort to comfort in Hungarian—one of the world's most difficult languages—shows extraordinary love that your partner will treasure forever.

Sajnálom (SHOY-nah-lohm) is the most important phrase to know. Use it whenever you need to communicate with family.

Cultural Tip

When video calling Hungarian family, addressing parents and elders with respect is important. Use formal greetings and show genuine interest in their lives.

More Useful Expressions

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Bocsáss meg Forgive me BOH-chahsh meg
Az én hibám It's my fault ahz AYN HEE-bahm
Nagyon sajnálom I'm very sorry NAH-dyon SHOY-nah-lohm

Jól látnak/hallanak? Can you see/hear me well? (Formal/Plural)

Pronunciation: YOHL LAHT-nahk/HAWL-lah-nahk?

"Jól látnak/hallanak? Jó a kapcsolat?"

Additional Phrases

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Jóvá akarom tenni I want to make it right YOH-vah AH-kah-rohm TEN-nee
Bocsánat Sorry BOH-chah-naht
Hibát követtem el I made a mistake HEE-baht KUH-vet-tem el

Navigating Formal Greetings

In Hungarian culture, the level of formality depends on age and relationship. When speaking with in-laws for the first time, using the formal "Ön" or the respectful "tetszik" construction is standard. Instead of a casual "How are you?", using a more respectful version shows you value their position in the family.

A common respectful greeting for elders is "Kezicsókolom," which literally means "I kiss your hand." While people rarely perform the physical gesture today, the phrase remains a staple of polite conversation with older generations.

Hogy vannak? How are you? (Formal/Plural)

Pronunciation: HOD-y VAHN-nahk?

"Hogy vannak? Meséljenek, mi történt Önökkel azóta."

Troubleshooting Technical Issues

Video calls often suffer from lag or audio drops. Knowing how to explain these issues in Hungarian prevents confusion and keeps the conversation flowing. If the connection becomes unstable, use specific terms for "frozen" screens or "choppy" audio.

If you cannot see the other person, say "Nem látom önöket." If the audio is the issue, "Nem hallom" is the quickest way to alert them. Clear communication during these glitches helps maintain the momentum of the call and reduces the stress of technical failure.

Nagyon örülök, hogy beszélhetünk! I'm very glad we can talk!

Pronunciation: NAH-dyon UH-ruh-luk, hohdy BE-sayl-he-tunk!

"Nagyon örülök, hogy beszélhetünk! Már régóta szerettem volna."

Meaningful Connection and Small Talk

Once greetings are finished, transitioning into light conversation helps build rapport. Asking about their health or the weather in Hungary are safe, traditional topics. If they look well, a compliment like "Jól tetszik kinézni" (You look well) is highly effective.

Expressing joy at seeing them through the screen bridges the distance. Using "Örülök, hogy látom önöket" (I am glad to see you) at the beginning or end of the call creates a warm atmosphere. This level of engagement goes beyond simple vocabulary; it signifies an investment in the family dynamic.

Viszontlátásra hamarosan! See you soon! (Formal)

Pronunciation: VEE-sont-LAHT-ahsh-rah HAH-mah-roh-shan!

"Köszönjük a beszélgetést, viszontlátásra hamarosan!"

Tips for Using These Phrases

  1. Test technology beforehand - Avoid awkward starts
  2. Have phrases ready - Keep this guide nearby
  3. Smile often - Body language transcends language barriers
  4. Ask questions - Show interest in their lives
  5. Be patient - Technical issues happen to everyone

Practice Makes Perfect

Learning to communicate with family in your partner's language shows dedication and care. Even if your pronunciation isn't perfect, the effort itself demonstrates your commitment to the relationship.

title: Hungarian Wedding and Anniversary Celebration Vocabulary
content_html:
Hungarian Wedding and Anniversary Celebration Vocabulary

Whether you're planning a Hungarian wedding, celebrating an anniversary with your Hungarian partner, or attending a Hungarian couple's special day, knowing the right vocabulary will help you participate fully in these joyous occasions. From proposals to golden anniversaries, this guide covers all the romantic vocabulary you need.

💕

Phrase to Learn

Hozzám jössz feleségül?

Will you marry me? (to a woman)

[ HOZ-zahm yös FE-le-shay-gül ]

The traditional marriage proposal question for men to ask women

The Proposal (A Lánykérés)

The magical moment when you ask your partner to marry you requires special vocabulary:

Lánykérés Marriage proposal (lit. asking for the girl)

Pronunciation: LAHNY-kay-raysh

"A lánykérés a Halászbástyánál volt."

Hozzám jössz? Will you marry me?

Pronunciation: HOZ-zahm yös

"Szeretlek. Hozzám jössz?"

Igen, akarok! Yes, I do! / Yes, I want to!

Pronunciation: I-gen AH-kah-rok

"Igen, akarok! Persze, hogy hozzád megyek!"

Proposal Vocabulary

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Eljegyzés Engagement EL-yedy-zaysh
Eljegyezni To get engaged EL-ye-dyez-ni
Jegygyűrű Engagement ring YEDY-dyű-rű
Gyémánt Diamond DYAY-mahnt
Letérdelni To kneel down LE-tayr-del-ni
Megkérni a kezét To ask for one's hand MEG-kayr-ni ah KE-zayt
Menyasszony Fiancée/Bride ME-nyahs-sony
Vőlegény Fiancé/Groom VŐ-le-gayny
🇭🇺

Hungarian Proposal Traditions

In traditional Hungarian culture, it was customary for the man to formally ask the woman's father for permission before proposing. While this is becoming less common in modern times, some couples still appreciate this respectful gesture. The proposal is called "lánykérés" (asking for the girl), reflecting these traditional roots.

Wedding Vocabulary (Esküvői Szókincs)

Hungarian weddings are extensive events that often last from early afternoon until the following morning. Navigating these celebrations requires a specialized lexicon that differentiates between the legal act of marriage and the social event that follows. This section introduces the core terminology necessary for identifying the participants and the legal requirements of a Hungarian union.

The vocabulary is organized to follow the chronological flow of a typical wedding day. You will first find "Essential Wedding Terms" to establish a foundation, followed by specific terms for the official proceedings in "The Ceremony (A Szertartás)." Finally, the list covers "The Reception (A Lakodalom)," focusing on the festive environment, traditional foods, and the lively atmosphere that defines a Hungarian wedding feast.

Házasság Marriage

Pronunciation: hah-zah-shahg

"A házasság egy fontos mérföldkő az életben."

Essential Wedding Terms

Esküvő Wedding

Pronunciation: ESH-kü-vő

"Az esküvőnk júniusban lesz."

Házastárs Spouse

Pronunciation: HAH-zahsh-tahrsh

"Ő az én házastársam."

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Feleség Wife FE-le-shayg
Férj Husband Fayrj
Menyasszony Bride ME-nyahs-sony
Vőlegény Groom VŐ-le-gayny
Tanú Witness TAH-nú
Koszorúslány Bridesmaid KO-so-rúsh-lahny
Vőfély Best man VŐ-fayly
Násznép Wedding guests NAHSS-nayp
Anyós Mother-in-law AH-nyósh
Após Father-in-law AH-pósh

The Ceremony (A Szertartás)

Polgári esküvő Civil wedding

Pronunciation: POL-gah-ri ESH-kü-vő

"Először polgári esküvőt tartunk."

Templomi esküvő Church wedding

Pronunciation: TEM-plo-mi ESH-kü-vő

"A templomi esküvő a nagymamának fontos."

Fogadom I do / I promise

Pronunciation: FO-gah-dom

"Igen, fogadom."

Ceremony Terms English Pronunciation
Eskü Vow ESH-kü
Házassági fogadalom Marriage vows HAH-zahs-shah-gi FO-gah-dah-lom
Karikagyűrű Wedding ring KAH-ri-kah-dyű-rű
Gyűrűcsere Ring exchange DYŰ-rű-che-re
Anyakönyvvezető Registrar AH-nyah-könyv-ve-ze-tő
Pap Priest Pahp
Oltár Altar OL-tahr
Mise Mass MI-she
Áldás Blessing AHL-dahsh
Csók Kiss Chók
🇭🇺

Two Ceremonies

In Hungary, couples often have two wedding ceremonies: a civil ceremony (polgári esküvő) at the town hall, which is legally required, and a religious ceremony (templomi esküvő) at church. The civil ceremony is typically smaller, with the church wedding being the grand celebration.

The Reception (A Lakodalom)

Lakodalom Wedding reception/feast

Pronunciation: LAH-ko-dah-lom

"A lakodalom reggelig tartott!"

Menyasszonytánc Bride's dance

Pronunciation: ME-nyahs-sony-tahnts

"A menyasszonytánc egy hagyomány."

Reception Vocabulary English Pronunciation
Esküvői torta Wedding cake ESH-kü-vő-i TOR-tah
Asztali beszéd Speech/Toast AHS-tah-li BE-sayd
Pohárköszöntő Toast PO-hahr-kö-sön-tő
Zenekar Band ZE-ne-kar
Első tánc First dance EL-ső tahnts
Virágcsokor Bouquet VI-rahg-cho-kor
Csokordobás Bouquet toss CHO-kor-do-bahsh
Menyasszonyi ruha Wedding dress ME-nyahs-sony-i RU-hah
Öltöny Suit ÖL-töny
Vendégek Guests VEN-day-gek

Wedding Wishes and Congratulations

Gratulálok az esküvőhöz! Congratulations on your wedding!

Pronunciation: GRAH-tu-lah-lok ahz ESH-kü-vő-höz

"Gratulálok az esküvőhöz! Nagyon boldogok vagytok!"

Sok boldogságot! Much happiness!

Pronunciation: Shok BOL-dog-shah-got

"Sok boldogságot kívánok nektek!"

Isten éltessen! God bless you! (Long life!)

Pronunciation: ISH-ten AYL-tesh-shen

"Isten éltessen benneteket!"

Wedding Wishes Table

Hungarian English When to Use
Boldog házasságot! Happy marriage! General wish
Sok boldogságot és szeretetet! Much happiness and love! Heartfelt wish
Éljetek boldogan! Live happily! Traditional
Örök szerelmet kívánok! I wish you eternal love! Romantic
Isten áldása legyen rajtatok! God's blessing upon you! Religious
Csodálatos életet kívánok együtt! Wishing you a wonderful life together! Warm wish

Anniversary Vocabulary (Évforduló)

Once the wedding festivities conclude, couples track their journey through yearly milestones known as anniversaries. In Hungarian, the word for anniversary is "évforduló," which literally translates to a "year-turn." Understanding how to describe these recurring celebrations is a key part of discussing long-term relationships and family history. This section outlines the basic nouns and adjectives used to identify different lengths of time and specific types of milestones.

Celebrating these dates often involves specific linguistic etiquette, depending on whether you are the one celebrating or a guest offering congratulations. The following subsections provide a breakdown of "Basic Anniversary Terms" for identifying the event, followed by a guide to "Anniversary Messages." These phrases range from formal congratulations to more personal sentiments suitable for greeting cards, social media posts, or toasts during a family dinner.

Házassági évforduló Wedding anniversary

Pronunciation: hah-zah-shah-gee ayv-for-doo-lo

"Ma van a szüleim huszadik házassági évfordulója."

Basic Anniversary Terms

Évfordulónk van It's our anniversary

Pronunciation: AYV-for-du-lónk vahn

"Ma évfordulónk van - öt éve házasodtunk."

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Első évforduló First anniversary EL-ső AYV-for-du-ló
Papíresküvő Paper wedding (1 year) PAH-pír-esh-kü-vő
Faesküvő Wood wedding (5 years) FAH-esh-kü-vő
Ónévforduló Tin anniversary (10 years) ÓN-ayv-for-du-ló
Kristályesküvő Crystal wedding (15 years) KRISH-tahy-esh-kü-vő
Porcelán esküvő Porcelain wedding (20 years) POR-tse-lahn ESH-kü-vő
Ezüstesküvő Silver wedding (25 years) E-züsht-esh-kü-vő
Gyöngyesküvő Pearl wedding (30 years) DYÖNDY-esh-kü-vő
Rubinesküvő Ruby wedding (40 years) RU-bin-esh-kü-vő
Aranyesküvő Golden wedding (50 years) AH-rahny-esh-kü-vő
Gyémántesküvő Diamond wedding (60 years) DYAY-mahnt-esh-kü-vő
🇭🇺

Hungarian Anniversary Names

Each anniversary has a traditional name in Hungarian, usually related to a material that symbolizes the strength and nature of the relationship at that stage. The "aranyesküvő" (golden wedding - 50 years) is particularly celebrated, often with a renewal of vows ceremony.

Anniversary Messages

Boldog évfordulót, szerelmem! Happy anniversary, my love!

Pronunciation: BOL-dog AYV-for-du-lót SE-rel-mem

"Boldog évfordulót, szerelmem! Ez a legjobb öt év volt!"

Köszönöm ezt a csodálatos tíz évet! Thank you for these wonderful X years!

Pronunciation: KÖ-sö-nöm EST ah CHO-dah-lah-tosh TEEZ AY-vet

"Köszönöm ezt a csodálatos tíz évet!"

Anniversary Message Translation
Mindig szeretlek I will always love you
Veled akarom tölteni az életemet I want to spend my life with you
Te vagy az életem szerelme You are the love of my life
Hálás vagyok minden napért veled I'm grateful for every day with you
Együtt öregedünk meg We'll grow old together
Újra és újra téged választanálak I'd choose you again and again

Romantic Celebration Phrases

Emlékszel az első találkozásunkra? Do you remember when we first met?

Pronunciation: EM-layk-sel ahz EL-ső TAH-lahl-ko-zah-shunk-rah

"Emlékszel az első találkozásunkra? Olyan ideges voltam!"

Ez a legszebb napom This is my most beautiful day

Pronunciation: Ez ah LEG-sebb NAH-pom

"Ez a legszebb napom, mert itt vagy velem."

Romantic Phrase Translation Occasion
Örökké szeretlek I will love you forever Wedding/Anniversary
A szívem örökre a tiéd My heart is forever yours Vows/Anniversary
Veled minden nap különleges Every day is special with you Any celebration
Megváltozott az életem, mióta megismertelek My life changed since I met you Anniversary
Te vagy a legjobb döntésem You are my best decision Wedding/Anniversary
Újra megtennék mindent I would do it all again Anniversary

Traditional Hungarian Wedding Customs

Understanding Hungarian wedding traditions will help you participate fully:

Menyasszonytánc (Bride's Dance)

Menyasszonytánc Bride's dance (money dance)

Pronunciation: ME-nyahs-sony-tahnts

"A menyasszonytáncnál a vendégek pénzt adnak a menyasszonynak."

During this tradition, male guests pay to dance with the bride, and the collected money goes to the newlyweds.

Vőfély (Master of Ceremonies)

Vőfély Traditional wedding MC/Best man

Pronunciation: VŐ-fayly

"A vőfély mondja a rigmusokat az esküvőn."

The vőfély traditionally leads the wedding festivities, reciting poems and verses throughout the celebration.

Traditional Phrases

Tradition Hungarian English
Before eating Jó étvágyat! Enjoy your meal!
Toast Egészségetekre! To your health!
Blessing Isten áldjon! God bless you!
Wishing well Sok boldogságot! Much happiness!
Traditional cheer Éljen az ifjú pár! Long live the newlyweds!
🇭🇺

The Wedding Feast

Traditional Hungarian weddings feature elaborate multi-course meals with dishes like töltött káposzta (stuffed cabbage), gulyás, and various roasted meats. The celebration often continues until dawn with music, dancing, and plenty of pálinka toasts!

Planning Your Celebration

Hová menjünk ünnepelni? Where should we go to celebrate?

Pronunciation: HO-vah MEN-yünk ÜN-ne-pel-ni

"Hová menjünk ünnepelni az évfordulónkat?"

Foglaljunk asztalt? Should we book a table?

Pronunciation: FOG-lahl-yunk AHS-tahlt

"Foglaljunk asztalt a kedvenc étteremünkben?"

Celebration Planning Vocabulary

Hungarian English Pronunciation
Meglepetés Surprise MEG-le-pe-taysh
Ajándék Gift AH-yahn-dayk
Virág Flowers VI-rahg
Pezsgő Champagne PEZ-gő
Romantikus vacsora Romantic dinner RO-mahn-ti-kush VAH-cho-rah
Gyertya Candle DYER-tyah
Ünnepelni To celebrate ÜN-ne-pel-ni
Emlékek Memories EM-lay-kek
Fénykép Photo FAYNY-kayp
Nászút Honeymoon NAHS-út

Sample Anniversary Conversation

Partner A: Boldog évfordulót, szerelmem! Happy anniversary, my love!

Partner B: Neked is! Nem hiszem el, hogy már öt éve házasok vagyunk! You too! I can't believe we've been married for five years!

Partner A: Emlékszem az esküvőnkre, mintha tegnap lett volna. I remember our wedding like it was yesterday.

Partner B: Olyan gyönyörű nap volt. És te olyan jóképű voltál az öltönyödben! It was such a beautiful day. And you looked so handsome in your suit!

Partner A: Te voltál a legszebb menyasszony. Köszönöm ezt az öt évet! You were the most beautiful bride. Thank you for these five years!

Partner B: Köszönöm neked! Veled akarom tölteni az életem hátralévő részét. Thank you! I want to spend the rest of my life with you.

Partner A: Szeretlek. Gyerünk ünnepelni! I love you. Let's go celebrate!

Quick Reference Card

Occasion Hungarian English
Proposal Hozzám jössz? Will you marry me?
Acceptance Igen! Yes!
At the altar Fogadom I do
Wedding wish Sok boldogságot! Much happiness!
Anniversary Boldog évfordulót! Happy anniversary!
Toast Egészségetekre! To your health!
Declaration Örökké szeretlek I'll love you forever

Your journey through love's milestones with your Hungarian partner will be enriched by knowing these special words and phrases. Whether you're saying "fogadom" at the altar or celebrating your aranyesküvő after 50 years together, these expressions will help you share in the joy of your Hungarian traditions.
